metadata.title: States PCS: Madhya Pradesh GK Questions For MPPSC Examinations

questions.question: Which district has the lowest sex ratio in Madhya Pradesh? (MPPSC State Service Prelims 2021)
questions.explanation: According to the 2011 Census, Bhind district in Madhya Pradesh has the lowest sex ratio of 837 females per 1000 males. Balaghat has the highest sex ratio in the state with 1021 females per 1000 males. The overall sex ratio of Madhya Pradesh in 2011 was 931 females per 1000 males. Other low sex ratio districts include Morena, Gwalior, Datia, and Shivpuri.

questions.question: Bt Brinjal is genetically modified for what purpose? (MPPSC State Services Prelims Exam 2022)
questions.explanation: Bt Brinjal is genetically modified by inserting the cry1Ac gene from Bacillus thuringiensis. This gene produces a toxin that targets insect pests, killing them when they ingest the plant tissue. The modification specifically protects brinjal from fruit and shoot borer larvae by disrupting their digestive system. Mahyco developed Bt Brinjal, the first genetically modified food crop approved for commercialization in India. The main trait conferred by Bt Brinjal is insect resistance.

questions.question: Which is incorrect about Madhya Pradesh’s climate? (MPPSC State Services 2023)

options.label: A
options.text: The climate of Madhya Pradesh is tropical.

options.label: B
options.text: The state has a monsoon climate.

options.label: C
options.text: Temperate climate is found in the southern part.

options.label: D
options.text: The Tropic of Cancer passes through central Madhya Pradesh.

answer.label: C
answer.text: Temperate climate is found in the southern part.

questions.explanation: Madhya Pradesh has a subtropical climate with winter, summer, and rainy seasons. It experiences monsoon climate influenced by southwest monsoon winds. The Tropic of Cancer passes through the central part of the state. The southern region mainly has a tropical wet and dry climate, not a temperate climate. Districts like Dhar lie in subtropical zones, receiving most rainfall during the southwest monsoon between June and September.

questions.question: Who led the 1857 revolt in Hoshangabad? (MPPSC Assistant Prof 4th Aug 2024 General Studies Paper I)
questions.explanation: Thakur Daulat Singh led the 1857 revolt in Hoshangabad. The 1857 revolt began in Meerut and spread across northern and central India. It is also called the First War of Indian Independence. The revolt opposed British colonial rule and included leaders like Rani Lakshmibai and Bahadur Shah II. Thakur Daulat Singh rallied local people in Hoshangabad against the British. The uprising ended East India Company rule and led to direct British Crown control of India.

questions.question: Which were the top two food-grain crops by area in Madhya Pradesh in 2018-19? (MPPSC Assistant Prof (Geography) Official Paper-II)
questions.explanation: Madhya Pradesh is one of India’s largest wheat producers, primarily grown during the rabi season from November to April. During 2018-19, gram (chickpea) was also a leading crop in area under cultivation. The state is the largest producer of gram in India, sown from October-November and harvested by March. These two crops dominated in terms of cultivated area among food grains in Madhya Pradesh during this period.

questions.question: Who built the Khajuraho temples in Nagarshaili style? (MPPSC Assistant Prof 2022)
questions.explanation: The Chandel dynasty ruled Central India from the 10th to 13th centuries. They built the Khajuraho temples, known for Nagarshaili architecture. These temples are a UNESCO World Heritage site with intricate sculptures. Construction occurred during rulers like Yashovarman and Dhanga. The Pal dynasty ruled Bengal and Bihar and supported Buddhism. The Pratihara dynasty controlled parts of northern India and resisted Arab invasions. The Pallava dynasty ruled Tamil Nadu and is known for rock-cut monuments at Mahabalipuram.

questions.question: Which Kalchuri ruler seized Kaliyanag’s golden statue and gave it to Somnath temple? (MPPSC Assistant Prof 2022 History Paper II)
questions.explanation: Lakshmanraj was a Kalachuri ruler who defeated the king of Odisha. He took the golden statue of Kaliyanag and donated it to the Somnath temple. This is recorded in historical accounts of the Kalachuri dynasty’s military and religious activities. Shankargana II, Yuvrajdev, and Gangeydev were also Kalachuri rulers but are not linked with this specific event.

questions.question: Which method is recognized for resolving industrial relations disputes? (MPPSC Assistant Prof 4th Aug 2024)
questions.explanation: Conciliation involves a neutral third party helping disputing parties reach agreement. Adjudication is a formal, legally binding judgment by an impartial third party. Arbitration submits a dispute to one or more arbitrators who make binding decisions. The government supports these methods under labour laws to maintain industrial peace. Various bodies and tribunals facilitate dispute resolution through conciliation, arbitration, and adjudication in India.

questions.question: Which medieval town was famous for printed cotton cloth called ‘Chinth’? (MPPSC State Service 2021)
questions.explanation: Sironj, located in Madhya Pradesh, was renowned for producing printed cotton cloth known as ‘Chinth’ during medieval times. The textile trade in medieval India included various regions famous for different fabrics, and Sironj was noted for its chintz textiles. Chintz is a printed cotton fabric with floral motifs, originating in India and traded widely. The Indian Ocean trade routes facilitated the exchange of such textiles. Sironj was specifically recognized for its chintz among other textile-producing towns in Madhya Pradesh.

questions.question: What is the annual Madhya Pradesh government amount under Kisan Kalyan Yojana?
questions.explanation: The Madhya Pradesh Mukhyamantri Kisan Kalyan Yojana provides Rs. 4,000 annually to farmers in addition to the Rs. 6,000 under the Central Government’s PM Kisan Samman Nidhi Yojana. Launched by CM Shivraj Singh Chauhan on 26 September 2020, about 80 lakh farmers benefit from this scheme. The state government deposited Rs. 800 crore in cooperative banks to support the scheme under the Revenue Department. The total assistance to farmers sums to Rs. 10,000 annually combining both schemes.
